bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. My highest utility values across all issues are: issueA=valueE (1.0), issueB=valueE (1.0), issueC=valueE (1.0), issueD=valueE (1.0), issueE=valueE (1.0). This gives me a utility of 1.0 across all weighted issues. Starting strong is appropriate at the beginning of negotiation with no time pressure yet.

The opponent opened with a very aggressive bid heavily favoring their own interests (low utility values for me across the board). At t=0.02, we're very early in the negotiation, so I should stay close to my top-utility bid. My opening was all valueE (max utility ~1.0). I'll make a very minor concession on the least important issues (issueA and issueC, both weight 0.1) while holding firm on issueE (weight 0.3), issueB and issueD (weight 0.2 each). I'll drop issueA to valueD and issueC to valueD to signal willingness to negotiate while maintaining high overall utility.

The opponent is holding firm on their low-utility offers (issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueD: valueA, issueE: valueA/valueB), showing no concession. It's very early (t=0.04), so I should stay close to my top utility while making minor concessions on low-weight issues. My highest weight issues are issueE (0.3), issueB (0.2), and issueD (0.2) — I'll hold firm on those. I can slightly concede on issueA and issueC (both weight 0.1) to signal willingness to negotiate.

The opponent is barely moving - they've offered nearly the same bid three times (mostly valueA across issues, with valueB for issueC and issueE sometimes). Their offers give me very low utility (~0.35). It's still very early (t=0.06), so I should stay firm on my high-importance issues (issueE weight 0.3, issueB and issueD weight 0.2 each) while making small concessions on lower-importance issues. I'll hold valueE on issueE, issueB, and issueD, and concede slightly on issueA and issueC (lower importance).

The opponent is stubbornly holding near their best offer (issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueA, issueE: valueA/valueB) with almost no concession. It's still very early (t=0.08), so I should stay firm on my high-importance issues (issueE weight 0.3, issueB and issueD weight 0.2 each) while making small concessions on low-importance issues. I'll keep issueE at valueE and issueD at valueE (my top priorities), and slightly adjust issueA and issueC which matter less to me.

The opponent is essentially stuck on valueA for issueA, issueB, issueD and alternating between valueA/valueB for issueE and valueB for issueC. Their offers give me very low utility (~0.35-0.38). My highest-weight issues are issueE (0.3), issueB (0.2), and issueD (0.2) - I should hold firm on these. The opponent seems to care a lot about issueA and issueD (always valueA), while I care less about issueA (weight 0.1). I'll concede issueA to valueA (low weight for me), keep issueE and issueD at high values, and make small concessions on issueB and issueC to signal willingness to negotiate while maintaining high utility.
